Hooper.gg — Desktop Audit

Home, Compete, Improve, Organize pages + a live walkthrough of the Hub login → subscribe flow. Every finding below includes the actual screenshot it's based on.

3Must fix
5Worth improving
7Already working
Red — change this, it's losing you customers
Blue — improve when you can, not urgent
Green — working well, leave it alone
🔴 Fix These First

1. The Hub is a two-step wall with no way out

Click "Hub" in the nav → generic login form → log in → a second, separate $11.99/mo paywall appears. Only exit is "Sign out." Your most curious visitors hit two walls and can't get back to your site.

Nav bar: the 'NEW' badge that starts the click
After login: a second paywall, not the product
Only way out: Sign out — no link back to the site

Fix: show pricing before login. Add a link back to the main site on every Hub screen.

2. Every page forces an app download — no lighter option

Every single CTA is "Download the App." A desktop visitor researching before committing has no lightweight next step — no demo, no email capture, nothing.

The only two buttons on every page, every time

Fix: add "watch a demo," "try the web version," or an email capture next to the app buttons.

3. A trust stat contradicts itself

Homepage: "1,000,000+ shots tracked." Hub login page: "2,000,000+ shots tracked." Same claim, two different numbers — right before someone hands over an email and password.

Homepage footer: 1,000,000+
Hub login page: 2,000,000+

Fix: one-line copy change. Pick one number, use it everywhere.

🔵 Worth Improving (Not Urgent)

4. Duplicate copy across pages

The same heading and paragraph appear on Home and Compete, word-for-word. Reads templated, and can quietly hurt Google rankings.

Home page version
Compete page — same heading, same paragraph

Fix: rewrite each instance for its specific page and audience.

5. The same layout repeats 5+ times

"Heading + three phone screenshots" shows up on Home, Compete, and Organize, more than once each. Visitors start skimming past it instead of reading what's different.

Compete page instance
Organize page — same structure again

Fix: vary the format — a single large screenshot, a short video, a stat callout.

6. Weakest testimonials are on the most important page

Homepage testimonials are anonymous handles. A far stronger, named NBA skills coach testimonial sits on the secondary Improve page instead.

Homepage: handles only, no names or roles
Improve page: named, credentialed — buried here instead

Fix: move the credentialed testimonial to the homepage.

7. Pricing tables use different words for the same thing

Homepage: "Free tier / Paid tier," "Recording." Hub subscribe page: "Basic / Pro," "Upload limit." Same tiers, different labels. (Price itself checks out: $11.99/mo, confirmed in both places.)

Homepage pricing table
Hub subscribe page — different labels, same features

Fix: match the wording exactly across both tables.

8. Small visual inconsistencies

Green starburst badges on the Organize page clash with the sharp, rectangular style used everywhere else. The Hub login step (not the rest of the site) uses a generic white template.

Off-brand rounded badge style
Generic white template vs. the site's dark branding

Fix: restyle the badges; reskin the login form to match the brand.

🟢 Already Working — Leave It Alone

Visual identity is strong and consistent — dark theme, athletic photography, bold type, across every marketing page.

Product screenshots show real UI, not vague mockups — genuinely informative.

Meta tags, Open Graph, and Twitter Cards are all correctly set up — link shares will render properly everywhere.

Credibility markers (a16z/Speedrun backing, MIT Sloan Sports Analytics Conference) are real and well-placed.

Page structure is smart — separate pages built for players, coaches, and organizations.

Subscription price is consistent everywhere it appears — $11.99/mo, verified on both the homepage and the Hub subscribe page.

The Hub subscribe page itself (not the login step before it) is actually on-brand — bold gradient, same heavy type as the rest of the site.

Scope: desktop only. Does not cover mobile/responsive behavior, performance testing, accessibility, or pages not reviewed (Pricing, For Camps/Leagues/Events, Contact, Privacy Policy).
